tasks.task_name: Find warm backpacking destination (1-2 night, mid-March)
tasks.first_domino: Google 'best warm weather backpacking mid-March USA'
tasks.hook_line: Where in the US can you actually camp in shorts in mid-March?
tasks.reward: Locks in the destination — makes flights and gear planning concrete
nuggets: Joshua Tree fully bookable 6 months out
Red Rock Canyon 2hr from Vegas airport
tasks.system_prompt: Help me find a warm backpacking destination for a 1-2 night trip the weekend of March 14-15, 2026. I'm flying from San Francisco and need somewhere warm enough to camp comfortably (ideally 60°F+ at night). It's for my friend's 30th birthday, so bonus points for scenic spots. After the trip we fly to Chicago on March 16th for the actual birthday celebration. I need destination options with flight logistics, trailhead info, and whether permits are required. Focus on Southwest US or similar warm regions.

tasks.first_domino: Open Google Flights and search SFO to [destination]
tasks.hook_line: 6 days out and no flights booked — prices won't get better
tasks.reward: Commits the trip — no more waffling on whether this is happening
nuggets: Book connecting flight same day as safety
Southwest lets you cancel for credit
tasks.system_prompt: I need to book flights from San Francisco to [destination] for a backpacking trip March 14-15, 2026. Flying out Friday March 14th morning/midday, returning Sunday March 15th evening. Then I have a separate flight to Chicago on March 16th. Show me best flight options on Google Flights or similar, prioritize reasonable times over absolute cheapest. I'm traveling with one friend (Mariano). Also advise on whether to book the backpacking return and Chicago departure as separate tickets or if there's a better routing.

tasks.task_name: Book flight from backpacking to Chicago
tasks.first_domino: Open Google Flights and search [backpacking city] to Chicago
tasks.hook_line: Chicago flight already booked — this just fills the gap between trips
tasks.reward: Closes the full travel loop for the entire birthday weekend
tasks.system_prompt: I need to book a flight from [backpacking destination city] to Chicago on March 16th, 2026. This connects a backpacking trip (ending March 15th evening) to a birthday celebration in Chicago (starting March 16th evening). Looking for flights that arrive Chicago by early/mid afternoon to give time to settle in. Show me best options and advise on how much buffer to leave after the backpacking trip ends.

tasks.task_name: Figure out REI gear rentals (backpack, tent, sleeping bag)
tasks.first_domino: Search 'REI gear rental San Francisco' and open their page
tasks.hook_line: REI rents full backpacking kits — you might not need to buy anything
tasks.reward: Answers the gear question so you can pack light and skip $500+ in purchases
nuggets: Reserve 2 weeks ahead for weekend trips
tasks.system_prompt: I'm planning a 1-2 night backpacking trip in mid-March 2026 (weekend of March 14-15) and don't own backpacking gear. I've heard REI rents full kits. Help me figure out: (1) What REI rents (backpack, tent, sleeping bag, pad?), (2) Pricing for a weekend rental, (3) How far in advance to book, (4) Which REI location in San Francisco to pick up from, (5) Whether it's worth renting vs buying for a one-off trip. The destination will be somewhere warm (60°F+ at night).

tasks.first_domino: Text Mariano's girlfriend to ask if group gift is planned
tasks.hook_line: What do you get someone turning 30 when you're already giving them a camping trip?
tasks.reward: Checks the gift box so you arrive in Chicago empty-handed guilt-free or thoughtfully prepared
tasks.system_prompt: My friend Mariano is turning 30 on March 19th. I'm already organizing a backpacking trip for his birthday weekend (March 14-15) and then meeting him in Chicago for the celebration. I'm debating whether to also get him a physical gift. Help me think through: (1) Is the trip itself the gift, or is a separate present expected for a 30th? (2) If I do get something, what's appropriate for a close friend's milestone birthday (~$50-150 range)? (3) Should I coordinate with his girlfriend or other friends on a group gift? Mariano likes outdoors, tech, food, and travel.

tasks.task_name: Look up eyeglasses stores in Kyoto for early trip shopping
tasks.first_domino: Google 'eyeglasses shops Kyoto English speaking optometrist'
tasks.hook_line: Japanese eyewear is legendary — where do locals actually buy glasses in Kyoto?
tasks.reward: First stop in Japan sorted — shop early, wear new glasses the whole trip
tasks.system_prompt: I'm traveling to Kyoto March 25-27, 2026 and want to buy eyeglasses there (planning to buy 4 pairs total during the Japan trip). Help me find eyeglasses stores in Kyoto that: (1) Have English-speaking staff or are foreigner-friendly, (2) Offer same-day or next-day service, (3) Are centrally located near hotels/temples, (4) Have good reputations for quality frames and lenses. I want to shop early in the trip so I can wear the new glasses. Provide store names, locations (Google Maps links), and any tips on the buying process in Japan.

tasks.task_name: Validate insurance/prescription requirements for buying 4 pairs of glasses
tasks.first_domino: Email insurance agent with subject 'Vision coverage for Japan purchases'
tasks.hook_line: Can you actually get 4 pairs reimbursed if you buy them all in Japan?
tasks.reward: Confirms you can buy guilt-free or adjusts the plan before you land
nuggets: Save receipts in English if possible
tasks.system_prompt: I have vision insurance (VSP or similar) and plan to buy 4 pairs of eyeglasses in Japan during a March 2026 trip. Help me figure out: (1) Do I need a current prescription from a US optometrist, or can I get an eye exam in Japan? (2) Will my insurance reimburse for glasses purchased abroad? (3) What documentation do I need to save for reimbursement (receipts, prescriptions)? (4) Are there limits on how many pairs I can claim in one year? (5) Any tips for making international eyewear purchases insurance-friendly? I want to confirm this is viable before committing to the plan.

tasks.first_domino: Open Google Maps and search 'Kyoto temples worth visiting'
tasks.hook_line: Kyoto has 2000+ temples — which ones are actually worth the early wake-up?
tasks.reward: Closes out Kyoto planning — only Takayama and Tokyo left on the itinerary
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan activities for 2.5 days in Kyoto (March 25-27, 2026). Traveling with my girlfriend Dana. We like temples, walking, outdoor spaces, and good food. Already have hotels booked in central Kyoto. I want a day-by-day itinerary with opening hours, Google Maps links, and a suggested walking route. Include a mix of major landmarks (Fushimi Inari, Kinkaku-ji, Arashiyama) and less touristy spots. Also flag best times to visit each place to avoid crowds. Note: We're also shopping for eyeglasses early in the trip, so factor in time for that.

tasks.first_domino: Google 'things to do Takayama 2 days spring'
tasks.hook_line: Takayama is the Alps stop nobody talks about — what's actually there?
tasks.reward: Fills in the mountain town gap between Kyoto and Tokyo
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan activities for 2 days in Takayama (late March/early April 2026). Traveling with Dana. We're coming from Kyoto and heading to Tokyo after. Takayama is our mountain town / slower pace break. Interested in: old town architecture, local food, morning markets, and potentially a day trip to Shirakawa-go. Give me a 2-day itinerary with timing, how to get around, and whether we need to book anything in advance. Also let me know if late March is a good time weather-wise.

tasks.first_domino: Google 'Tokyo neighborhoods guide first time visitor'
tasks.hook_line: Tokyo is massive — which neighborhoods actually matter for a first-timer?
tasks.reward: Locks in the grand finale — the trip is fully planned front to back
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan activities for 4-5 days in Tokyo (early April 2026). Traveling with Dana. We've already been to Kyoto and Takayama, so Tokyo is the big city finale. Interests: food markets, unique neighborhoods, art/culture, fountain pen shopping (limited edition Sailor pens), watch shopping (Grand Seiko), and eyeglasses shopping (need to buy 2-3 more pairs). Want a mix of iconic Tokyo experiences and local discoveries. Give me a neighborhood-based itinerary with must-see spots, Google Maps links, and recommendations on where to stay (we haven't booked Tokyo hotels yet). Also flag anything that needs advance booking.

tasks.first_domino: Open VS Code and navigate to the task-pilot project folder
tasks.hook_line: You've already scaffolded the extension — now make it actually do something
tasks.reward: Gets the MVP functional so you can dogfood it in your own Cursor workflow
nuggets: VS Code extension API well documented
Local markdown as state = simple start
tasks.system_prompt: I'm building a VS Code extension called 'AI Task Pilot' to help with AI-assisted development while maintaining task focus. The goal: visualize work in progress and provide AI with context of ongoing work while editing a shared document locally. It's an alternative to my Craft agent system which doesn't integrate well with Cursor projects. I've already scaffolded the extension. Help me continue development on core features: (1) Reading/writing a local markdown task file, (2) Displaying tasks in a VS Code sidebar view, (3) Syncing task state when the markdown file changes. Use TypeScript and VS Code extension APIs. Assume I'm familiar with coding but new to VS Code extension development.

tasks.first_domino: Sketch 3 different sidebar layouts on paper or Excalidraw
tasks.hook_line: How do you show task context without cluttering a code editor?
tasks.reward: Clarifies what the extension actually looks like — easier to build with a target
tasks.system_prompt: I'm designing the UI for a VS Code extension that visualizes active tasks while coding. The extension reads from a local markdown file and needs to show: (1) Current active task with context, (2) List of other tasks in the file, (3) Quick actions like mark done or switch tasks. Constraints: Must fit in a VS Code sidebar without overwhelming the code view. The target user (me) has ADHD and gets distracted easily, so clarity and low visual noise matter. Help me design 2-3 UI options using VS Code's webview or TreeView APIs. Show mockups or describe the layout in detail.

tasks.first_domino: Write pseudocode for how AI gets current task context
tasks.hook_line: Can you pipe task context into Cursor's AI without breaking the flow?
tasks.reward: The core value prop — AI that knows what you're working on without manual prompting
nuggets: Cursor uses LSP for code context
VS Code commands can trigger AI prompts
tasks.system_prompt: I'm building a VS Code extension that provides AI (specifically Cursor's AI) with context about the current task I'm working on. The task context lives in a local markdown file. I want the AI to automatically know what I'm focused on when I ask it questions, without me having to manually paste context every time. Help me design an AI context management system: (1) How to inject task context into Cursor's AI (clipboard, command palette, custom prompt prefix?), (2) What metadata to include (task description, related files, recent changes?), (3) How to keep it unobtrusive (automatic vs manual trigger?). Use TypeScript and VS Code APIs. Prioritize simplicity for MVP — I can iterate later.

recommended_slots.reason: Weekend ramp_up + 6-day deadline makes this urgent; low context cost fits lower energy state
Quick research win that batches well with other Japan planning; low effort palette cleanser
Novel REI rental research is curiosity-driven and low context cost; pairs with slot 1 for backpacking momentum
